sanskrit: श्रद्धया परया तप्तं तपस्तत्त्रिविधं नरैः। अफलाकाङ्क्षिभिर्युक्तैः सात्त्विकं परिचक्षते॥
transliteration: śraddhayā parayā taptaṃ tapas tattri-vidhaṃ naraiḥ / aphalākāṅkṣibhir yuktaiḥ sāttvikaṃ paricakṣate
literal_meaning: That three-fold austerity which is practiced with supreme faith by persons without desire for fruits is declared to be of the nature of goodness.
contextual_meaning: Authentic self-discipline arises when action is offered as service rather than as a transaction for personal gain.
psychological_state: Flow-state: disciplined effort free of egoic score-keeping
trigger_scenario: Training for a marathon solely to raise funds for a cause you believe in
anchor_text: Discipline done faithfully without craving reward carries pure goodness.
